text: Robust #cybersecurity is a collective effort. Let’s do our part to protect the House and our personal data. RT #Cyber116thCongress & stay tuned for tips & best practices. https://t.co/Bk06ccRmle
#BeCyberSmart. When creating #passwords, choose unique, unidentifiable phrases, characters, and numbers unrelated to the House or your office. #Cyber116thCongress

1/ This article proves that the CIA whistleblower's claims about procedures to safeguard transcripts of POTUS phone calls with foreign officials is false.
https://t.co/2PME3Bja7E

2/Read this: "The Trump White House upgraded the security of the National Security Council’s top-secret codeword system in the spring of 2018, according to two former Trump White House officials familiar with the matter, as part of an effort to ferret out and deter leaks."
3/ So we now know this did NOT occur after the transcript of the Ukraine call was written up.

4/ I didn't know the timing of this security upgrade since I started at the NSC in late May 2018.  I knew these transcripts were tightly controlled and that steps to do this started in the spring of 2017 after at least 2 of them were leaked.

5/This was an appropriate decision to protect sensitive information at great risk of being leaked. The president is free to do this since he has ultimate classification authority.  The whistleblower's charge that the transcripts were "locked down" as part of a coverup is a fraud.

1/ The NYT confirmed what I said last week: Schiff knew about the CIA whistleblower in advance -- way in advance.  Before he even filed his complaint. This is a much bigger scandal that people realize.  At a minimum, Schiff should recuse himself from this impeachment inquiry.

Below is this twitter thread and the two relevant tweets:

https://t.co/fzwLXchBFD https://t.co/tBVLT0HY3r
3/ So the whistleblower DID come to the @HouseIntelComm first just as i said.  We now know that the request by the whistleblower to meet with committee members was a fraud -- he already did this.

@HouseIntelComm 4/ Supposedly the @HouseIntelComm dem counsel told the CIA whistleblower to hire counsel and go through the ICIG process.  That would be believable if Schiff and his staff revealed this weeks ago.  @Susan_Hennessey and @MiekeEoyang : don't be fooled by this.

@HouseIntelComm @Susan_Hennessey @MiekeEoyang 5/ Schiff talked about the Ukraine issue throughout the month of August.  The contents of the complaint leaked before it was presented to Congress.  There is zero chance in mind mine that Schiff's HPSCI lawyers were not working hand and glove with the WB and his lawyers.

@HouseIntelComm @Susan_Hennessey @MiekeEoyang 6/ And here's the kicker (@Susan_Hennessey &
@MiekeEoyang pay attention): under @HouseIntelComm rules, any classified info brought to the committee from outside sources MUST BE SHARED WITH BOTH SIDES.  Schiff broke committee rules by not telling committee GOP members about this.

@HouseIntelComm @Susan_Hennessey @MiekeEoyang 7/ This is a very serious violation of @HouseIntelComm rules.  Why did Schiff do this, especially when he was using this information to score political points throughout the month of August?  The reason is clear: it was part of the latest Dem ploy to take down @realDonaldTrump

@HouseIntelComm @Susan_Hennessey @MiekeEoyang @realDonaldTrump 8/ It never made any sense to discuss impeachment or a non-intelligence matter like the Ukraine allegations in @HouseIntelComm .  Given Schiff's politicization of this issue and serious violations of HPSCI rules, the impeachment charade must end.

You were pretty hard on me last night on @CNN last night @ChrisCuomo when I told you that I assessed @RepAdamSchiff probably knew about the CIA whistleblower in advance.  We now know from the NYT that I was right.  Apology not necessary.  @realDonaldTrump https://t.co/4mui9BCvFz

In #Baghdad where my hotel has received security orders banning #journalists from staying in rooms facing river or on high floors where you can see Tahrir Square protest unfolding. Transported to Saddam’s time when entire hotel wings were blocked so we couldn’t see things.

At #Baghdad protest today when I could open my eyes again after a cloud of tear gas descended, a policeman with a bunch of plastic roses with tiny white teddy bears handed me one. And I think that pretty much explains why #journalism is the best job in the world.
